ks1.objective: Pass the cone with your partner. When your colour is called, race back to your home line.
successCriteria: Watches and listens for the colour call
Reacts quickly when their colour is called

lks2.objective: Develop sharp reactions and quick first-step speed under coach calls.
successCriteria: Pre-loads on the balls of the feet
Plants and turns in one motion
Stays composed when calls switch quickly

uks2.objective: Combine reaction speed with dribbling under pressure — react to the coach while protecting the football.
successCriteria: Reacts without losing control of the ball
Uses a turn or feint before sprinting

space.description: Pairs face each other with two different coloured cones. A 'home line' is marked behind each player.

value.name: Respect
value.text: Respect is shown through how we listen, react and play fairly. Encourage children to accept outcomes without arguing and stay positive whether they win or lose each round.

steps: Organise children into pairs facing each other. Each pair has two different coloured cones. Mark a home line behind each player.
Pairs continuously pass the cones to each other, swapping which cone they're holding.
At any moment, the coach calls out a colour.
The player holding that cone must turn and run back to their home line.
Their partner reacts and tries to tag them before they get there.
Reset quickly and continue once the round is complete.
progressions: Introduce footballs so players must dribble while passing cones.
Children start on their knees when passing the cone, then jump up and run home.
Rotate pairs regularly for different challenges.
Switch the rule — children run if they HAVEN'T got the called colour instead of if they have.
questions: What position helps you react quickest?
How can you read your partner's next move?
What's the trade-off between standing tall and being ready to sprint?

step.space: Increase the distance to the home line to challenge speed
step.task: Children must react using a specific movement or turn before running
step.equipment: Add more cones or colours to increase decision making
step.people: Change pairings regularly to expose children to different opponents

base.showmanship: Use clear, loud calls and quick restarts to maintain tempo. Praise children who react quickly and accept outcomes positively — reinforcing good behaviour as well as performance.
